Goal model: Convert orange ring to purple. Hypothesis: clicking orange ring pixels when white diamond is at purple ring will convert them.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on orange ring pixel to test conversion
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 37}])

# Check the result
print(f"Game state: {last_action_result.get('state', 'N/A')}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', 'N/A')}")
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed', 'N/A')}")

# Check the current frame segmentation
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Count orange and purple pixels in the ring area (rows 48-54, cols 37-43)
orange_count = 0
purple_count = 0
for node in nodes:
    if node['boundary']:
        for p in node['boundary']:
            row, col = p
            if 48 <= row <= 54 and 37 <= col <= 43:
                if node['color'] == 'O':
                    orange_count += 1
                elif node['color'] == 'p':
                    purple_count += 1

print(f"Orange pixels in ring area: {orange_count}")
print(f"Purple pixels in ring area: {purple_count}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
